### Post by kstruve on 2007-04-29
The weirdest thing just happened that I can't figure out.  For some reason, my windows manager isn't loading when I start Ubuntu.  I'm using Feisty.  I haven't changed anything or installed anything lately, so I don't know what could be causing this.  Windows manager loads just fine in Failsafe Gnome mode and also from the live cd.  Any thoughts?  Here is my xorg.conf:

# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Mon Feb 26 23:38:46 PST 2007

### Post by starcraft.man on 2007-04-29
I'm gonna guess you had a problem with your install of nvidia driver... just switch the driver to the 2d "nv" driver.

```
sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
```

When you get to the part that says video driver, choose nv, not nvidia.

I hope I read your question right. I believe failsafe loads ya up in the 2d drivers by default that's probably why it worked.

---

### Post by kstruve on 2007-04-29
I have had problems with my Nvidia drivers, but I thought I had got them working correctly.  I had problems right after upgrading to feisty, but then I used the let ubuntu load the newest drivers for me and it was working fine.  Then out of the blue today, none of my windows have top bars.  So, use "nv" instead of "nvidia" in my xorg.conf?

---

### Post by starcraft.man on 2007-04-29
> **kstruve said:**
> I have had problems with my Nvidia drivers, but I thought I had got them working correctly.  I had problems right after upgrading to feisty, but then I used the let ubuntu load the newest drivers for me and it was working fine.  Then out of the blue today, none of my windows have top bars.  So, use "nv" instead of "nvidia" in my xorg.conf?

Hmmm, I heard quite a bit of trouble from people who left their Nvidia drivers in when upgrading to feisty. Had you already had your 3d drivers in and left them? If so, there may be more problems than just your graphics driver on your install. It is highly recommended to do a clean install.

My recommendation would be to switch to the nv and boot into your drive, save your info to a seperate space or external drive (unless you smartly partitioned /home to a seperate partition). Then reinstall your feisty. I always believe in clean installs, upgrades always leave room for problems, especially in this instance if you left in the Nvidia drivers ><. Hope ya get it fixed.

---
